title: Jiaasjen
content:
{{Top|leg|italics=1}}
'''''Jiaasjen''''' was a religious theory and ideal espoused by the [[Jensaarai/Legends|Jensaarai]] movement, and originated with [[Larad Noon]]. The term was assumed to be a [[Sith (language)|Sith]] phrase meaning "integrating the shadow", and referred to the concept of integrating the teachings of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]] into usage of the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side]]. It was similar to [[Count of Serenno/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku's]] belief that a perfect being could draw on both sides of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] equally.

==History==
''Jiaasjen'' theory was developed by [[Larad Noon]], one of the survivors of the [[Brotherhood of the Sith]], who felt great guilt for his misdeeds during the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]. Noon developed the theory in an attempt to rationalize his wrongdoings, and to keep himself from going insane. He wrote extensively on ''Jiaasjen'' during his self-imposed exile on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Susevfi]] until his death.

Several thousand [[Standard year/Legends|years]] later, during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], [[Jensaarai/Legends|Jensaarai]] followers of the recently-deceased [[Nikkos Tyris]] led by a [[Unidentified female Saarai-kaar|female Saarai-kaar]] arrived on Susevfi in search of [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] teachings. They happened upon Noon's writings on "integrating the shadow" and, with no other teacher to follow, the [[Saarai-kaar]] integrated his work into her own, resulting in a [[The Force/Legends|Force]] cult that was neither wholly light nor wholly dark in nature.
